title: Charred Silky Japanese Puff Agedashi
description: A quick-fix Japanese dish featuring Thai red curry paste, lamination puff, and a halal-friendly twist. Agedashi is a popular Japanese street food that's perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan or a deep fryer to 375°F (190°C).
Cut the lamination puff pastry into small squares, about 2 inches (5 cm) per side.
Cook the puff pastry squares in batches until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Drain the fried pastry on paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together the Thai red curry paste, soy sauce, sesame oil, and kombu dashi powder.
Heat the curry paste mixture in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the green onions and ginger to the curry paste mixture and cook for 1 minute.
Pour the hot broth over the fried puff pastry squares.
Add the curry paste mixture and sesame seeds (if using) to the puff pastry.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Note: For an extra crispy bottom, you can pan-fry the puff pastry for 1-2 minutes before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
